question: What does the !important declaration do in CSS?
options: Marks the property as a reminder for the developer
Doubles the specificity of the rule
Overrides all other declarations for that property, regardless of specificity
Makes the property apply to all child elements

question: A rule is written as: div.container > p.intro — how is its specificity calculated?
options: One ID, no classes, no elements → (1, 0, 0)
No IDs, two classes, two elements → (0, 2, 2)
No IDs, one class, two elements → (0, 1, 2)
No IDs, two classes, one element → (0, 2, 1)
